meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Stop the cycle of unfinished apps. Learn how to use portfolio building to ship faster, validate ideas, and generate revenue using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from perfectionism and scope creep. By shifting to a 'Portfolio Building' mindset, you treat every MVP as a modular asset rather than a monolith. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to automate boilerplate, enforce strict time-boxing, and ship functional, revenue-ready micro-SaaS tools in days, not months.
content.intro:
Your desktop is a graveyard of half-finished repositories. You have the vision, the technical skill, and the drive, yet every project hits the 'murky middle'—that phase where the initial adrenaline fades and the reality of maintenance sets in. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you are building monoliths instead of a portfolio.

True founders don't build one perfect product; they build a portfolio of high-velocity micro-SaaS assets. By treating your codebase as a modular investment, you strip away the paralysis of perfection. Today, we replace the endless cycle of abandonment with a systematic, AI-accelerated workflow that forces you to ship, iterate, and monetize.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Value Proposition
steps.description: Narrow your scope to a single, high-value problem that can be solved in 48 hours. Focus on one core feature that solves a pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this problem: [INSERT PROBLEM]. Propose a 48-hour MVP scope that focuses on one core feature for a micro-SaaS. Define the schema, the primary user flow, and the monetization model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a project roadmap based on the micro-SaaS scope defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Foundation with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate. Stop configuring build tools and start writing business logic immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Next.js and Supabase boilerplate. Include authentication, Stripe integration, and a responsive Tailwind layout. Ensure the code is production-ready.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file ./scaffold.json --action 'Initialize project structure and install dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic via Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your core business logic while maintaining a clean separation of concerns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic: [INSERT CORE LOGIC]. Ensure it is modular and decoupled from the UI components for easier testing and future reuse.
steps.claude_code_command: claude chat 'Implement the business logic for the core feature, ensuring it is unit-tested and decoupled from UI.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment and Feedback Loops
steps.description: Set up automated deployment pipelines so you can ship to production in one command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for this project that runs tests and deploys to Vercel on every push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Vercel deployment and GitHub Actions for continuous integration.'

steps.title: Monetization Integration
steps.description: Wire up your payment gateway to ensure you are ready to collect revenue the moment the product goes live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to manage user subscriptions and update the database status to 'active' once payment is confirmed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --task 'Integrate Stripe API and setup webhook handlers for subscription management.'

pro_tips: Ship the 'ugly' version first; use Claude Code to polish the UI only after you have verified the core logic works.
Always maintain a library of reusable UI components; your portfolio is only as fast as your ability to copy-paste successful modules.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests alongside your code; skipping tests is the #1 reason founders get stuck in refactoring hell.
Cap your build time at 72 hours; if it takes longer, you are building a product, not an asset.
Focus on the 'Time to First Dollar' metric; if you aren't integrating payments by day 3, you are hobbyist coding, not building a business.
content.next_steps: After completing your first micro-SaaS, deploy it to a directory like Product Hunt or your own landing page. Gather feedback for 7 days, then repeat the process to build your second asset.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop the cycle of abandonment. Don't just code; build assets that work for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
